title: Iran Bank Branch Network Size Over Time -- Compiled Timeseries (1919-2016)
source_org: Compiled by this project from multiple secondary/academic sources (curated, hand-compiled -- see docs/bookkeeping.md rule 5)
retrieved_by: claude (hand-compiled from cross-checked secondary/academic sources, each row individually cited in the source column)
time_coverage: 1919, 1940, 1954, 1977, 1978, 1982, 2004, 2005, 2016 (sparse spot-years, not continuous -- this is what the underlying sources actually provide)
units: count (branches, banks) and ratio (branches per million population)
license_terms: compiled from multiple sources cited per-row
notes: Directly addresses the mission's 'number of bank branches nationally over time' ask. Genuinely continuous annual branch-count data was NOT found for any source this round (would require Bank Markazi Annual Reports for every intervening year, most of which are not digitized/accessible -- see also the pre-existing data/raw/pahlavi-era-primary-extraction/ Bank Markazi/World-Bank extractions, which cover different individual years via primary-document transcription, not this compiled-narrative approach). This dataset should be read as a set of verified anchor points (each individually sourced), not an interpolatable continuous series -- no values were invented to fill gaps between rows. Combined with the pre-existing data/raw/pahlavi-era-primary-extraction/wb1960-banking-statistics-private-sector-1957-1959/ (deposit/credit levels for June 1957-June 1959) and the new data/raw/worldbank-gfdd/iran-banking-sector-depth-1960-2016/ (banking-depth RATIOS 1960-2016, this same round), the project now has a reasonably well-triangulated picture of Iran's banking-system SIZE (branches/bank-count, sparse anchors) and DEPTH (deposits/credit-to-GDP ratios, continuous annual) even though a true year-by-year branch count remains elusive.
